wines.name: Clos Mogador
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barrels, mix of new and used

pairings.why: Rich dark fruit and firm tannins balance the fat and smoke.
Garrigue aromatics echo the lamb's herbaceous character.
The mineral core and acidity cut through sheep's milk richness.

history.summary: Clos Mogador was first produced in 1989 by René Barbier, one of five pioneers who revived winemaking in Priorat after decades of decline. The estate covers approximately 18 hectares of old Garnacha and Cariñena vines on llicorella slate soils in the village of Gratallops.

milestones.event: First vintage of Clos Mogador, part of the founding cooperative effort with the other Cinc Pioners
Priorat elevated to DOQ status, one of only two Spanish regions with this classification
René Barbier Jr. joins the estate, continuing the family winemaking tradition

wines.description: The estate's flagship red from 18 hectares of old Garnacha and Cariñena vines on llicorella slate in Gratallops, first produced in 1989.

wines.name: Clos Nelin
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in French oak barrels, 8-10 months on lees

pairings.why: Saline mineral character echoes the sea while the texture matches the shellfish richness.
The wine's full body and herbal notes complement the chicken's fat and skin.
Barrel fermentation and nutty lees character harmonize with truffle's earthiness.

history.summary: Clos Nelin was created by René Barbier to explore the potential of old white varieties on Priorat's llicorella soils. The blend is based on old Garnacha Blanca vines complemented by Macabeo and Pedro Ximenez.

milestones.event: First vintage of Clos Nelin, one of Priorat's earliest white wines from the modern era

wines.description: White blend from old Garnacha Blanca, Macabeo, and Pedro Ximenez on llicorella slate, barrel-fermented and aged on lees. One of Priorat's rare whites.

wines.name: Manyetes
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French oak barrels, 16-18 months

pairings.why: The wine's minerality and firm tannins pair with the lamb's richness and the herb crust.
Dark fruit and graphite notes echo the char and herb sauce.
Structured tannins and earthy character match the game's intensity.

history.summary: Manyetes is named after the Manyetes vineyard in Gratallops, one of the historic plots cultivated by Álvaro Palacios since he established his Priorat estate in 1989. The wine is a Vinya Classificada single-vineyard expression based on old Cariñena vines.

milestones.event: First bottling of Manyetes as a standalone single-vineyard Vinya Classificada

wines.description: Single-vineyard Vinya Classificada from old Cariñena vines in the Manyetes plot, Gratallops. Structured, mineral, and herb-inflected.

wines.name: L'Ermita
wines.oak_regime: 16-18 months in new French oak barrels

pairings.why: The wine's concentration and firm tannins find a match in tender slow-roasted lamb.
Mineral depth and intensity complement the complexity of cured Iberian pork.
The wine's earthy mineral character pairs naturally with truffle.

history.summary: L'Ermita takes its name from a small hermitage chapel at the top of the vineyard in Bellmunt del Priorat. Álvaro Palacios acquired the plot in the early 1990s and first bottled it separately in 1993. The vineyard spans roughly one hectare of century-old Garnacha vines on near-vertical llicorella terraces.

milestones.event: First vintage of L'Ermita as a separate bottling
Designated as a Gran Vinya Classificada under the DOQ Priorat classification system

wines.description: Gran Vinya Classificada from a single hectare of century-old Garnacha on steep llicorella terraces in Bellmunt del Priorat. First vintage 1993.

wines.name: Finca Dofí
wines.oak_regime: 14-16 months in French oak, mix of new and used

pairings.why: Classic Garnacha and lamb pairing, the wine's fruit and minerals match the char and fat.
The wine's density and structure meet the gamey richness of the meat.
Earthy mountain goat cheese finds a match in the wine's mineral and earthy register.

history.summary: Finca Dofí was the wine that preceded L'Ermita as Álvaro Palacios's flagship expression, first made in 1992. It draws from several old-vine plots across the Gratallops subzone and remains one of the estate's most important wines.

milestones.event: First vintage of Finca Dofí as an estate single-vineyard wine

wines.description: Single-vineyard Garnacha-dominant blend from old vines in Gratallops, first produced in 1992 by Álvaro Palacios.

wines.name: Gratallops Vi de Vila
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, mostly used

pairings.why: The spice and fruit of the wine complement the paprika-forward sausage.
The wine's medium weight and acidity balance garlic-rich chicken.
Cherry fruit and mineral freshness pair well with cured meats.

history.summary: The Gratallops Vi de Vila was introduced as the village-level tier of the Álvaro Palacios Priorat range, reflecting grapes sourced from across the Gratallops municipality. It occupies the tier between the entry-level Camins del Priorat and the single-vineyard wines.

milestones.event: First vintage of Gratallops Vi de Vila as a defined village-tier wine

wines.description: Village-level Garnacha and Cariñena blend from Gratallops, the approachable mid-tier expression in the Álvaro Palacios Priorat range.

wines.name: Camins del Priorat
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel and used oak, short ageing

pairings.why: Versatile fruit and medium structure pair easily with pizza toppings.

pairings.dish: Pasta with tomato and beef ragu
pairings.why: The wine's acidity and fruit mirror the tomato while matching the meat.
Herbal notes and fruit pair well with charred vegetables and nutty romesco sauce.

history.summary: Camins del Priorat was created as an entry-level expression to make the DOQ Priorat designation approachable for a wider audience. The name means 'paths of Priorat' in Catalan, reflecting the blend's sourcing from across the region.

milestones.event: First vintage of Camins del Priorat as the estate's entry-level Priorat wine

wines.description: Entry-level DOQ Priorat blend from Álvaro Palacios. Garnacha-forward with herbal freshness and mineral character, first made in 1999.

wines.name: Camins del Priorat Blanc
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation, minimal oak contact

pairings.why: The wine's salinity and citrus freshness mirror and amplify the seafood.
Fresh acidity and mineral character work with the cod's salinity.
The wine's herbal freshness and acidity balance the richness of alioli.

history.summary: The white version of Camins del Priorat was added to the range to show the potential of Priorat's old white varieties, particularly Garnacha Blanca, which thrives on the region's llicorella soils.

milestones.event: First vintage of Camins del Priorat Blanc introduced

wines.description: Entry-level white blend of Garnacha Blanca, Macabeo, and Pedro Ximenez from Álvaro Palacios, fresh and mineral.

wines.name: Clos Erasmus
wines.oak_regime: 16-18 months in French oak barriques, high proportion new

pairings.why: The wine's richness and structure meet the fat and skin of roasted pork.
Dark fruit and earthy mineral notes complement the game and mushroom earthiness.
The wine's concentration and cedary depth work with a firm, nutty aged cheese.

history.summary: Clos Erasmus was created by Daphne Glorian, who arrived in Priorat in the early 1990s as part of the wave of producers drawn by René Barbier's pioneering work. She established her own tiny estate in Gratallops and first bottled Clos Erasmus under her own label in 1994.

milestones.event: First bottling of Clos Erasmus as a standalone label by Daphne Glorian
DOQ Priorat elevation reinforces the appellation status of the wine

wines.description: Garnacha-dominant flagship from Daphne Glorian's tiny estate in Gratallops, first made in 1994. Rich, concentrated, and mineral.

wines.name: Laurel
wines.oak_regime: 12-14 months in French oak, mix of new and used

pairings.why: Garnacha fruit and herbal notes are a natural match for herb-roasted lamb.
Spice in the wine echoes the seasoning of the sausage.

pairings.dish: Catalan pa amb tomaquet with ham
pairings.why: Medium weight and acidity complement the simple, flavourful Catalan snack.

history.summary: Laurel was introduced as the second wine of Clos Erasmus to make the estate's Priorat character accessible at a lower price point while Daphne Glorian concentrates the selection for the flagship.

milestones.event: First vintage of Laurel as the second wine of Clos Erasmus

wines.description: Second wine of Clos Erasmus, Garnacha-led with Cariñena and Syrah, offering approachable Gratallops character.

wines.name: Clos Martinet
wines.oak_regime: 14-16 months in French and American oak barrels

pairings.why: Firm tannins and herbal character pair beautifully with slow-braised lamb.
The wine's density and mineral intensity match prime grilled beef.
Mineral freshness and tannins cut through the cheese's salty richness.

history.summary: Clos Martinet was founded by Josep Lluis Perez, a pioneering winemaker who was part of the Cinc Pioners group that relaunched Priorat in 1989. His daughter Sara Perez has led winemaking at the estate since 2000 and also co-founded Venus la Universal with René Barbier Jr.

milestones.event: Josep Lluis Perez joins the Cinc Pioners project and produces the first Clos Martinet
Sara Perez takes increasing responsibility for winemaking at Mas Martinet

wines.description: The flagship from Mas Martinet, a Garnacha-dominant blend from old vines in Falset. First produced in 1989 by Josep Lluis Perez.

wines.name: Camí Pesseroles
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, mostly used

pairings.why: Fruit and medium structure complement duck's richness and fig sweetness.
The wine's Garnacha fruit pairs naturally with the spiced pork sausage.
Earthy mineral notes echo the mushroom's depth.

history.summary: Cami Pesseroles is named after a historical path that crossed the Mas Martinet estate, used for centuries by donkeys carrying grapes from the hillside vineyards. It represents the mid-tier expression of the estate, between the entry-level and the flagship Clos Martinet.

milestones.event: First vintage of Cami Pesseroles as a distinct cuvee

wines.description: Mid-tier Garnacha and Cariñena blend from Mas Martinet, named for the historic mule path through the estate's vineyards.

wines.name: Martinet Bru
wines.oak_regime: Short ageing in used French oak

pairings.why: Herbal Garnacha character is a natural companion to spiced lamb.
Medium body and fresh acidity work with charred vegetables and nutty sauce.
The wine's freshness and fruit complement simple Catalan table foods.

history.summary: Martinet Bru was created as the entry-level wine from Mas Martinet to offer an approachable introduction to Priorat from one of the region's pioneering estates.

milestones.event: First vintage of Martinet Bru as the entry-level wine of Mas Martinet

wines.description: Entry-level Garnacha, Syrah, and Merlot blend from Mas Martinet. Fresh, herbal, and approachable, first made in 1995.

wines.name: Clos de l'Obac
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French oak barrels

pairings.why: Dark fruit and garrigue aromatics are a classic match with herb-crusted lamb.
Earthy mineral notes complement the mushroom's depth.

pairings.dish: Roast beef with red wine jus
pairings.why: Concentration and structure pair well with a classic roast.

history.summary: Clos de l'Obac is the flagship wine of Costers del Siurana, the estate established by Carles Pastrana as one of the five original Cinc Pioners producers who relaunched Priorat winemaking in 1989. The name refers to the shaded (obac) slopes of the estate.

milestones.event: Carles Pastrana founds Costers del Siurana and produces the first Clos de l'Obac

wines.description: Flagship Garnacha-dominant blend from Carles Pastrana's Costers del Siurana estate, one of the founding Cinc Pioners wines from 1989.

wines.name: Miserere
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

pairings.why: The wine's accessible fruit and medium structure work with the hearty pork and bean dish.
Ripe fruit and gentle spice complement the paprika seasoning.
Medium body and mild tannins work with charred vegetables.

history.summary: Miserere was created by Costers del Siurana as a multi-varietal blend positioned as a more accessible companion to the flagship Clos de l'Obac. The name pays homage to the region's Carthusian monastic heritage.

milestones.event: First vintage of Miserere introduced as the second-tier wine from Costers del Siurana

wines.description: Multi-varietal blend from Costers del Siurana, accessible companion to Clos de l'Obac. First made in 1993.

wines.name: Kyrie
wines.oak_regime: Barrel-fermented with extended lees contact

pairings.why: The wine's mineral texture and richness match the dense flesh of monkfish.

pairings.dish: Salt cod with olive oil and garlic
pairings.why: Salinity in both wine and dish create a natural harmony.
The wine's herbal and mineral notes complement the asparagus bitterness.

history.summary: Kyrie was produced by Costers del Siurana to showcase the potential of Priorat's indigenous white varieties on llicorella soils. The name continues the estate's practice of referencing Gregorian chant and Carthusian liturgy.

milestones.event: First vintage of Kyrie as the white wine from Costers del Siurana

wines.description: Barrel-fermented white blend of Garnacha Blanca, Macabeo, and Pedro Ximenez from Costers del Siurana. Textured and mineral.

wines.name: Scala Dei Negre
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak

pairings.why: The fruit and medium tannins work with the rich, fatty pork.
Cherry fruit and mild mineral note are a natural match with spiced lamb.
Medium body and acidity pair well with char and nutty sauce.

history.summary: Scala Dei Negre is produced by Cellers de Scala Dei, the estate adjacent to the ruins of the Carthusian monastery of Scala Dei, founded in the 12th century. The winery has been operating continuously since 1973, making it one of the earliest modern Priorat wineries.

milestones.event: Modern Cellers de Scala Dei established adjacent to the 12th-century Carthusian monastery
First commercially available vintage of Scala Dei Negre

wines.description: Entry-level Garnacha and Cariñena red from Priorat's historic Scala Dei estate. Accessible and fruit-forward.

wines.name: Scala Dei Cartoixa
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barrels, mix of new and used

pairings.dish: Roast leg of lamb with garlic
pairings.why: Structure and herbal notes complement slow-roasted lamb.
Earthy mineral depth and concentration find a match in game and truffle.
Tannin and mineral acidity cut through the fat of aged cheeses.

history.summary: Scala Dei Cartoixa is the Reserva expression from Cellers de Scala Dei, named for the Carthusian order (Cartoixa in Catalan) whose monastery sits adjacent to the estate. It represents the estate's commitment to expressing the historic terroir of Priorat.

milestones.event: First vintage of Scala Dei Cartoixa as the Reserva tier

wines.description: Reserva-level Garnacha-dominant blend from the historic Scala Dei estate adjacent to the 12th-century Carthusian monastery.

wines.name: Scala Dei Prior
wines.oak_regime: 20 months in new French oak barrels

pairings.why: Concentration and dark fruit intensity match venison's richness.

pairings.dish: Grilled T-bone steak with bone marrow
pairings.why: Firm tannins and mineral depth find their match in prime beef.
The wine's density and structure match the complexity of aged cured pork.

history.summary: Prior is the premium single-vineyard expression from Cellers de Scala Dei, named for the prior who led the Carthusian monastery adjacent to the estate. It represents the estate's highest selection from old-vine plots.

milestones.event: First vintage of Scala Dei Prior as the prestige cuvee

wines.description: Prestige cuvee from Cellers de Scala Dei, named for the prior of the historic Carthusian monastery. First made in 2001.

wines.name: Scala Dei Masdéu
wines.oak_regime: 22 months in new French oak barrels

pairings.why: Old-vine Garnacha concentration pairs well with delicate roasted lamb.
The wine's mineral depth and fruit concentration match the richness.

history.summary: Masdeu is a single-vineyard expression from Cellers de Scala Dei from the Masdeu plot, one of the oldest cultivated sites associated with the original Carthusian monastery. It focuses on pure old-vine Garnacha Tinta.

milestones.event: First vintage of Scala Dei Masdeu as a single-vineyard bottling

wines.description: Single-vineyard old-vine Garnacha from the historic Masdeu plot at Cellers de Scala Dei.

wines.name: Scala Dei Pla dels Angels
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation, no oak

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and mineral note complement the clean, delicate fish.
The wine's citrus and fennel character echo the mussel cooking liquid.

history.summary: Pla dels Angels is the white wine from Scala Dei, named for one of the estate's historic plots. Garnacha Blanca and Macabeo are the traditional white varieties grown alongside the red varieties in Priorat.

milestones.event: First vintage of Scala Dei Pla dels Angels white wine

wines.description: White blend of Garnacha Blanca and Macabeo from Scala Dei, stainless-steel fermented for freshness. First made in 1998.

wines.name: Les Manyes
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old French oak foudres

pairings.why: The wine's red fruit transparency and minerality pair with delicate game bird.

pairings.dish: Lamb shoulder with garlic and herbs
pairings.why: Fine tannins and fresh acidity balance the richness without overpowering.
The wine's mineral freshness and acidity cut through goat cheese richness.

history.summary: Les Manyes is the Garnacha Vinya Classificada from Dominik Huber's biodynamic estate Terroir Al Limit. Huber, a German-born winemaker who previously worked at Mas Martinet, arrived in Priorat in the early 2000s and developed a unique approach inspired by Burgundy, using whole bunches, large wood, and non-interventionist winemaking.

milestones.event: Dominik Huber establishes Terroir Al Limit in Priorat
First vintage of Les Manyes as a designated Vinya Classificada

wines.description: Old-vine Garnacha Vinya Classificada from the Les Manyes plot, biodynamically farmed by Dominik Huber. Translucent, mineral, and Burgundy-inspired.

wines.name: Les Tosses
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old French oak foudres

pairings.why: Firm tannins and mineral depth find their balance in rich slow-cooked beef.
Earthy mineral character echoes the earthiness of mushroom and game.
The firm structure and saline mineral note cut through aged cheese fat.

history.summary: Les Tosses is the Cariñena Vinya Classificada sibling to Les Manyes at Terroir Al Limit. Old-vine Cariñena on llicorella slate, harvested and vinified with Dominik Huber's signature whole-cluster, large-wood methodology.

milestones.event: First vintage of Les Tosses as a designated Vinya Classificada from old Cariñena vines

wines.description: Old-vine Cariñena Vinya Classificada from Les Tosses, biodynamically farmed by Dominik Huber at Terroir Al Limit.

wines.name: Dits del Terra
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old French oak foudres

pairings.why: The wine's structure and mineral freshness match slow-cooked lamb and earthy chickpeas.
Fruit and mineral depth balance fatty, flame-cooked pork.
The savoury mineral character complements this traditional Catalan braised dish.

history.summary: Dits del Terra ('fingers of the earth') is the mid-tier blend from Terroir Al Limit, combining Garnacha and Cariñena from the estate's biodynamically farmed plots in Gratallops. It represents Dominik Huber's Burgundy-inspired philosophy applied to a blend rather than a single variety.

milestones.event: First vintage of Dits del Terra as the blend wine from Terroir Al Limit

wines.description: Garnacha and Cariñena blend from Terroir Al Limit, farmed biodynamically and aged in large old foudres. First made in 2003.

wines.name: Terroir Sense Fronteres
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old French oak, minimal intervention

pairings.why: The wine's light body and freshness work unusually well with grilled fish.
Medium-weight Garnacha and poultry is a classic, versatile pairing.

pairings.dish: Catalan bread with olive oil and tomato
pairings.why: Simple, fresh food matches the wine's approachable, unadorned style.

history.summary: Terroir Sense Fronteres ('terroir without borders') was added to the Terroir Al Limit range as an accessible introduction to the estate's biodynamic Garnacha philosophy. The name reflects Dominik Huber's view that great terroir transcends classification boundaries.

milestones.event: First vintage of Terroir Sense Fronteres as the estate's entry expression

wines.description: Entry-level biodynamic Garnacha from Terroir Al Limit, light and fresh with mineral character. First made in 2009.

wines.name: Pedra de Guix
wines.oak_regime: Fermented in old French oak barrels, extended lees ageing

pairings.why: The wine's intense salinity and mineral edge match the oceanic, briny sea urchin.
Racy acidity and mineral freshness are a natural with fine oysters.
The wine's texture and mineral depth complement the richness of langoustine.

history.summary: Pedra de Guix is the white wine flagship from Terroir Al Limit, drawing on old Pedro Ximenez and Garnacha Blanca vines on Priorat's limestone-slate soils. The wine is aged in old barrels on extended lees, seeking mineral expression over oak influence.

milestones.event: First vintage of Pedra de Guix as the white flagship from Terroir Al Limit

wines.description: Old-vine white blend of Pedro Ximenez, Garnacha Blanca, and Macabeo from Terroir Al Limit. Intensely mineral and racy.

wines.name: Arbossar
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old French oak foudres

pairings.why: Firm Cariñena tannins are softened by long-braised collagen-rich beef.
Dark fruit and mineral freshness complement the herb sauce and fat.
Tannin grip and mineral acidity balance the sweet and salty cheese contrast.

history.summary: Arbossar is a Cariñena-focused expression from Terroir Al Limit, drawing from one of the estate's old-vine plots. The wine shows the variety's naturally high acidity and firm tannin structure expressed through Dominik Huber's minimal-intervention approach.

milestones.event: First vintage of Arbossar as a standalone Cariñena expression from Terroir Al Limit

wines.description: Old-vine Cariñena from the Arbossar plot at Terroir Al Limit, aged in large foudres. First made in 2001.

wines.name: Mas de la Rosa
wines.oak_regime: 18-24 months in new French oak barrels

taste.summary: The prestige single-vineyard wine from Vall Llach, sourced from the old Mas de la Rosa estate acquired in 1998. Dominated by old Cariñena with Garnacha, aged extensively in new oak.

pairings.dish: Roast rack of lamb with herbs
pairings.why: Concentration and firm tannins find a match in prime lamb.

pairings.dish: Braised wild boar with black truffle
pairings.why: Dense dark fruit and mineral depth complement the game and truffle.

history.summary: Mas de la Rosa is the prestige cuvee from Vall Llach, established by Catalan singer Lluis Llach and his partner Joan Manel Serrat in 1997. The wine comes from a separate historic estate acquired to provide old-vine Cariñena and Garnacha.

milestones.event: Lluis Llach and partners establish Vall Llach in Porrera
Mas de la Rosa estate acquired to add old-vine Cariñena to the portfolio
First vintage of Mas de la Rosa as a prestige single-vineyard wine

wines.description: Prestige single-vineyard old-vine Cariñena and Garnacha from Vall Llach. First made in 2001.

wines.name: Idus de Vall Llach
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in French oak, mix of new and used

taste.summary: Mid-tier blend from Vall Llach combining Cariñena with Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. Structured and fruit-forward with oak integration and mineral freshness.

pairings.why: Fruit and firm tannins match the richness of Iberian pork.
The wine's spice and acidity complement the tomato-based sauce.
Tannin structure and dark fruit balance salty aged cheese.

history.summary: Idus de Vall Llach is the mid-tier expression from the estate, blending the estate's Cariñena with international varieties. The name continues the Latin/classical naming convention used by Vall Llach.

milestones.event: First vintage of Idus de Vall Llach produced

wines.description: Mid-tier Cariñena, Merlot, and Cabernet Sauvignon blend from Vall Llach. First made in 1998.

wines.name: Embruix de Vall Llach
wines.oak_regime: 10-12 months in French and American oak, used

taste.summary: The entry-level five-variety blend from Vall Llach, offering an approachable introduction to the estate's Priorat style. Fruit-forward and rounded with the estate's characteristic mineral note.

pairings.why: Medium weight and fruit pair comfortably with simple roast poultry.

pairings.dish: Grilled Catalan sausage with white beans
pairings.why: The wine's accessible fruit and mild structure complement the classic dish.
Fresh acidity and fruit mirror the sweetness and char of the vegetables.

history.summary: Embruix ('spell' in Catalan) was introduced as the entry-level multi-variety blend from Vall Llach to offer an approachable Priorat at a more accessible price point. It draws from the estate's vineyards across the Porrera area.

milestones.event: First vintage of Embruix de Vall Llach as the entry-level blend

wines.description: Entry-level five-variety blend from Vall Llach, approachable and fruit-forward. First made in 2001.

wines.name: Aigüestortes
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, used

pairings.why: The wine's acidity and cherry fruit pair naturally with tender braised lamb.
Mineral freshness and earthy tannins complement the earthy mushroom-sausage combination.

history.summary: Aigüestortes is a mid-range Garnacha and Cariñena blend from Vall Llach named after the Catalan national park. It represents the estate's Porrera Village wine tier.

milestones.event: First vintage of Aigüestortes from Vall Llach

wines.description: Mid-tier Garnacha and Cariñena blend from Vall Llach's Porrera vineyards, named after the Catalan national park.

wines.name: Porrera Vi de Vila
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, used

pairings.why: The freshness and medium weight balance the fat of Iberian pork cuts.

pairings.dish: Roast chicken with garlic and herbs
pairings.why: Village-level Garnacha and poultry is a reliable, crowd-pleasing match.

pairings.dish: Catalan pa amb tomaquet with charcuterie
pairings.why: Simple acidity and mineral character complement the classic Catalan spread.

history.summary: Porrera Vi de Vila was introduced by Vall Llach to express the distinct character of the Porrera village zone, where the estate is based. Porrera is one of Priorat's cooler, higher subzones known for more structured, mineral wines.

milestones.event: First vintage of Porrera Vi de Vila from Vall Llach, reflecting the village subzone designation

wines.description: Village-designated Garnacha and Cariñena from the Porrera subzone at Vall Llach. Fresh, mineral, and structured.

wines.name: Salanca
wines.oak_regime: Barrel-fermented with lees ageing in used French oak

pairings.dish: Grilled sea bream with lemon oil
pairings.why: Mineral freshness and citrus in the wine complement the clean fish.
Medium body and herbaceous notes work with vegetable-based dishes.

history.summary: Salanca is the white wine from Vall Llach, added to the estate's range to reflect the potential of Priorat's indigenous white varieties. The wine is based on old-vine Garnacha Blanca and Macabeo from the estate's highest-elevation plots.

milestones.event: First vintage of Salanca as the white wine from Vall Llach

wines.description: Barrel-fermented white blend of Garnacha Blanca and Macabeo from Vall Llach's Porrera vineyards.

wines.name: Mas Doix Cariñena Centenaria
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barrels, new and used

pairings.dish: Roast lamb with anchovies and herbs
pairings.why: The wine's iron mineral character is amplified by the salty anchovies alongside the lamb.
Old-vine Cariñena concentration and firm tannins meet gamey hare.

history.summary: The 1902 Cariñena Centenaria takes its name from the planting year of the oldest vines on the Mas Doix estate. These century-old Cariñena vines were identified by the Doix-Rovira family as worth preserving and bottling separately when they relaunched the estate in the early 2000s.

milestones.event: Original Cariñena vines planted on the Mas Doix estate
First separate bottling of the centenarian Cariñena vines

wines.description: Pure Cariñena from century-old vines planted in 1902 at Mas Doix. First separate bottling in 2002.

wines.name: Doix Costers de Vinyes Velles
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barrels, mix of new and used

pairings.dish: Slow-roasted leg of lamb with garlic
pairings.why: The wine's density and structure are built for slow-cooked lamb.

pairings.dish: Grilled Iberian beef with bone marrow
pairings.why: Concentration and dark fruit match prime grilled beef.
Earthy mineral notes work with truffle and mushroom earthiness.

history.summary: Doix Costers de Vinyes Velles is the flagship blend from Mas Doix, the estate revived by the Doix family in the early 2000s in Poboleda. The name translates to 'old-vine hillside slopes', capturing the estate's terroir on steep llicorella terraces.

milestones.event: Doix-Rovira family relaunches Mas Doix after decades of dormancy
First vintage of Doix Costers de Vinyes Velles as the flagship

wines.description: Old-vine Cariñena and Garnacha flagship from Mas Doix, first made in 2004. Dense, mineral, and age-worthy.

wines.name: Salanques
wines.oak_regime: 12-14 months in French oak, mostly used

history.summary: Nou Naix ('new birth') is the second wine from Celler Cal Pla, offering a more approachable entry into the estate's Porrera style for early drinking.

milestones.event: First vintage of Nou Naix as the accessible second wine from Celler Cal Pla

wines.description: Accessible second wine of Garnacha and Cariñena from Celler Cal Pla, named 'new birth' in Catalan.

wines.name: L'Infernal Riu
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French oak, used

pairings.why: Fruit and herbal freshness complement herb-spiced lamb.
The wine's accessible fruit and spice pair with the simple dish.

history.summary: L'Infernal is a joint project established in Torroja del Priorat by three French winemakers from the northern Rhone Valley, drawn to Priorat's llicorella terroir. Riu is their entry-level Priorat expression.

milestones.event: L'Infernal project established by Combier, Fischer, and Gerin in Priorat
First vintage of Riu as the entry-level expression from L'Infernal

wines.description: Entry-level Garnacha, Cariñena, and Syrah from the French winemakers' Priorat project L'Infernal.

wines.name: L'Infernal
wines.oak_regime: 16-18 months in French oak barrels, mix of new and used

pairings.dish: Slow-roasted shoulder of lamb with herbs
pairings.why: Dense fruit and firm tannins are built for long-roasted lamb.

pairings.dish: Grilled venison with juniper berry sauce
pairings.why: The wine's gamey pepper notes and concentration match venison.

history.summary: L'Infernal (the flagship) was first produced in 2003, when the three French founders released their premium Priorat expression. The name refers to the intense heat and challenging conditions of making wine in Priorat's steep, arid terroir.

milestones.event: Three French winemakers establish the L'Infernal partnership in Priorat
First vintage of the flagship L'Infernal wine

wines.description: Flagship wine from L'Infernal, the Rhone winemakers' Priorat project. First made in 2003.

wines.name: Onix Fusió
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak, mix of used

taste.summary: A fruit-forward blend from Vinícola del Priorat combining Garnacha, Cariñena, and Merlot. Approachable and round with a soft texture.

pairings.why: Round, soft fruit and mild tannins complement roasted pork and fruit.
Accessible fruit and light body pair with the rich meat sauce.

history.summary: Onix Fusió was introduced by Vinícola del Priorat to occupy the space between the entry-level Classic and the evolving Evolució, offering a fruit-focused, immediately approachable style.

milestones.event: First vintage of Onix Fusió from Vinícola del Priorat

wines.description: Fruit-forward, approachable Garnacha, Cariñena, and Merlot blend from the Vinícola del Priorat cooperative.

wines.name: Clos Martinet Blanc
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in old French oak barrels, extended lees

pairings.why: The wine's texture and mineral richness pair with the firm, dense fish.
Full body and mineral salinity work with the richness of lobster rice.

history.summary: Clos Martinet Blanc was created by Sara Perez to show the full potential of Priorat's indigenous white varieties under biodynamic farming. It is aged in old barrels on lees for texture and mineral complexity.

milestones.event: First vintage of Clos Martinet Blanc from Mas Martinet

wines.description: Barrel-aged white of Garnacha Blanca, Macabeo, and Pedro Ximenez from Mas Martinet. Textured and mineral.

wines.name: Espectacle del Montsant
wines.oak_regime: Aged in large old French oak foudres and barrels

pairings.why: The wine's depth and mineral concentration match long-roasted lamb.

pairings.dish: Roast duck with fig and pepper sauce
pairings.why: Old-vine Garnacha fruit and mineral depth balance the duck richness.

history.summary: Espectacle del Montsant is a joint project between René Barbier of Clos Mogador and the Roqueta winery, produced from century-old Garnacha vines in the Montsant appellation surrounding Priorat. It focuses on a single plot of extreme old-vine Garnacha. Note: classified as DO Montsant, not DOQ Priorat.

milestones.event: First vintage of Espectacle del Montsant from the Barbier-Roqueta partnership

wines.description: Joint project of René Barbier and Roqueta family from century-old Garnacha vines in DO Montsant. First made in 2002.

wines.name: El Serral
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak

pairings.why: Garnacha fruit and mineral freshness pair with lamb's richness.
Medium body and fresh acidity complement the hearty legume dish.

history.summary: El Serral is the mid-tier wine from the Sangenís i Vaqué family estate, sourced from old-vine plots on the cooler slopes around Porrera.

milestones.event: First vintage of El Serral from Sangenís i Vaqué

wines.description: Mid-tier Garnacha and Cariñena from Sangenís i Vaqué in Porrera. Fresh, mineral, and approachable.

wines.name: Rotllan Torra Crianza
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in American and French oak, used

pairings.why: Medium body and fruit complement mildly spiced poultry.
Accessible fruit and medium tannins pair with grilled pork ribs.

history.summary: Rotllan Torra Crianza is one of the estate's foundational wines, produced since the mid-1990s. It is the accessible daily-drinking tier from the Torroja del Priorat estate.

milestones.event: First commercial vintage of Rotllan Torra Crianza

wines.description: Entry-level Crianza from Rotllan Torra. Garnacha, Cariñena, and Cabernet Sauvignon, accessible and fruit-forward.

wines.name: Bodegas Mas Alta Garnacha
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak

pairings.why: Classic Garnacha and lamb pairing.
Fresh, medium-bodied Garnacha pairs easily with rustic Catalan food.

history.summary: This varietal Garnacha from Bodegas Mas Alta was introduced to offer a more accessible expression of the estate's high-altitude Garnacha character.

milestones.event: First vintage of the varietal Garnacha from Bodegas Mas Alta

wines.description: Pure varietal Garnacha from high-altitude old vines at Bodegas Mas Alta. Fresh and mineral.

wines.name: Blanc de Roques
wines.oak_regime: Stainless steel fermentation, no oak

pairings.why: Fresh acidity and mineral note complement clean grilled fish.
Citrus freshness and mineral character work with the asparagus and rich sauce.

history.summary: Blanc de Roques ('white from rocks' in Catalan) is the white wine from Costers del Priorat, designed to show the mineral, fresh character of Priorat's white varieties from its slate soils.

milestones.event: First vintage of Blanc de Roques from Costers del Priorat

wines.description: Stainless-steel-fermented white blend from Costers del Priorat. Fresh and mineral.

wines.name: Cims de Porrera Vi de Vila
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in used French oak

pairings.why: Easy-going fruit and medium tannins are a versatile companion to the mixed grill.
The wine's fruit and mineral freshness pair with cured meats.

history.summary: Cims de Porrera introduced the Vi de Vila tier to complement its prestige Solanes and Classic ranges with a more accessible village-level expression from the Porrera appellation.

milestones.event: First vintage of Cims de Porrera Vi de Vila

wines.description: Village-level Cariñena and Garnacha from Cims de Porrera under the Porrera Vi de Vila designation.

wines.name: Clos Figueres
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barrique and foudre

pairings.why: The slate minerality and firm tannins complement charred lamb fat; romesco echoes the herb notes.
Salty crystalline cheese amplifies the wine's red-fruit lift and cuts through its density.
Rich pork fat and crisp skin find balance in the wine's acidity and mineral grip.

history.summary: Clos Figueres is the estate flagship of Clos Figueras, Christopher Cannan's Priorat project. Cannan, a Scottish wine merchant, established the bodega in the late 1990s, sourcing old Garnacha and Cariñena parcels across the DOQ.

milestones.event: First vintage of Clos Figueres produced from Priorat llicorella parcels
Estate expanded with additional old-vine Garnacha and Syrah parcels in Falset area

wines.description: Flagship red from Clos Figueras in the DOQ Priorat, blending Garnacha, Cariñena, Syrah and Cabernet Sauvignon from llicorella slate parcels near Falset.

wines.name: Font de la Figuera
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak barriques

pairings.why: Classic Catalan bread brightened with olive oil mirrors the wine's rustic red-fruit register.
Peppery Catalan pork sausage harmonises with the wine's herb and spice notes.
Smoky roasted peppers and aubergine complement the wine's earthy mineral character.

history.summary: Font de la Figuera was introduced as the approachable entry tier of Clos Figueras, designed to express DOQ Priorat character at a more accessible price point while drawing from the same old-vine parcels.

milestones.event: First Font de la Figuera vintage released as the estate's entry-level red

wines.description: Entry-level DOQ Priorat red from Clos Figueras, offering Garnacha and Cariñena fruit with slate minerality and garrigue at an accessible price.

wines.name: Nit de les Animes
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in large used French foudres and concrete

pairings.why: Earthy mushroom and lamb mirror the wine's mineral and savoury profile.
Crystalline nutty cheese finds resonance with the wine's bitter cherry and iron notes.
Game meat echoes the wild herb and graphite register of this natural-leaning wine.

history.summary: Ester Nin and Carles Ortiz founded their Porrera project in 2003, vinifying old Cariñena and Garnacha vines with minimal intervention. Nit de les Animes, meaning Night of the Souls, is the estate's principal red expression.

milestones.event: First vintage of Nit de les Animes produced in Porrera

wines.description: The flagship red of Nin-Ortiz in Porrera, crafted from organically farmed old Cariñena and Garnacha vines with indigenous yeast fermentation and minimal sulphur addition.

wines.name: Coma Blanca
wines.oak_regime: Aged in used oak barrique and amphora

pairings.why: Saline mineral and lemon notes complement the shellfish; alioli bridges to the wine's texture.

pairings.dish: Bacalao a la llauna (baked salt cod)
pairings.why: The wine's preserved-fruit and almond register matches the umami depth of cured fish.
Bitter herbaceous asparagus and hazelnut sauce echo the wine's savoury, nutty depth.

history.summary: Coma Blanca was introduced by Nin-Ortiz to explore the white wine potential of DOQ Priorat, drawing on Garnacha Blanca and Macabeo vines in Porrera with the same minimal-intervention philosophy as the estate red.

milestones.event: First Coma Blanca vintage produced as the estate's white wine expression

wines.description: Rare white DOQ Priorat from Nin-Ortiz in Porrera, blending old Garnacha Blanca and Macabeo with minimal intervention and amphora ageing.

wines.name: Salmos
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and American oak barrique

pairings.dish: Roast Iberian pork with romesco sauce
pairings.why: Fatty Iberian pork and the almond-pepper sauce match the wine's ripe fruit and spiced oak.
Charcoal-grilled lamb enhances the wine's dark-fruit and mineral character.
Salty aged Spanish cheese amplifies Garnacha's strawberry-and-spice register.

history.summary: Familia Torres, the Catalan wine producer founded in Vilafranca del Penedes in 1870, entered the Priorat appellation in 2001, planting old Garnacha and Cariñena parcels in Gratallops. Salmos, first released in 2004, represents the estate's approachable Priorat expression.

milestones.event: Familia Torres acquires vineyard land in Gratallops, DOQ Priorat
First Salmos vintage released

wines.description: The entry-level Priorat red from Torres Salmos in Gratallops, blending Garnacha, Cariñena and Syrah from the Torres family's DOQ Priorat estate.

wines.name: Perpetual
wines.oak_regime: 18 months in French oak barrique, 30% new

pairings.why: The wine's iron, spice and high tannins match the slow-cooked richness of oxtail.
Earthy Catalan mushroom rice echoes the wine's mineral and herb complexity.
Crystalline aged Mahon amplifies the wine's ferrous mineral thread.

history.summary: Perpetual is Torres Salmos's premium Priorat cuvee, based on old Cariñena vines of 60-80 years with small amounts of Garnacha. The name references the perpetual cycle of the Priorat terroir.

milestones.event: First Perpetual vintage released as Torres Salmos premium tier

wines.description: Premium DOQ Priorat from Torres Salmos, based primarily on old Cariñena vines in Gratallops with characteristic mineral grip and cellar potential.

wines.name: Venus la Universal
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak barrique and foudre

pairings.dish: Grilled cod with black olive tapenade
pairings.why: The wine's saline mineral and herb notes complement Mediterranean fish.
Catalan garlic chicken with tomato echoes the wine's red fruit and herb register.
Cured meats pair naturally with the wine's red-fruit and spice character.

history.summary: Sara Perez and Rene Barbier Jr launched Venus la Universal in 1999, focusing on organic Garnacha and Cariñena from the DO Montsant zone around Falset. The Venus red is their principal bottling from this cooler appellation.

milestones.event: Venus la Universal project founded by Sara Perez and Rene Barbier Jr
First Venus la Universal vintage released

wines.description: The flagship DO Montsant red from Sara Perez and Rene Barbier Jr's Venus la Universal project, blending organically farmed Cariñena, Garnacha and Syrah from Falset.

wines.name: Dido Negre
wines.oak_regime: 8 months in used French oak

pairings.why: The wine's garrigue herbs and fresh acidity pair beautifully with charred Mediterranean vegetables.

pairings.dish: Tapas of jamon iberico and olives
pairings.why: Salty cured meat and olive oil complement the wine's earthy, peppery character.
Tomato and mozzarella echo the fresh red fruit and mineral freshness of this entry-level Montsant.

history.summary: Dido Negre was introduced by Venus la Universal as an accessible entry-level red from DO Montsant. The name references the Carthaginian queen Dido, founder of Carthage, a nod to the Iberian Mediterranean heritage.

milestones.event: Dido Negre first released as the entry-level red of Venus la Universal

wines.description: Approachable entry-level DO Montsant red from Venus la Universal, offering organic Garnacha, Cariñena and Syrah with bright red fruit and fresh herbs.

wines.name: Dido Blanc
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in used oak barrique

pairings.dish: Grilled sea bass with lemon and herbs
pairings.why: Citrus and mineral notes complement delicate white fish seasoned with Mediterranean herbs.
Saline Montsant white mirrors the oceanic depth of Catalan seafood rice.
The wine's almond and citrus notes play off the tangy freshness of goat's cheese.

history.summary: Dido Blanc was added to the Venus la Universal range to offer a white counterpart from native Catalan varieties grown in the DO Montsant zone under organic certification.

milestones.event: First Dido Blanc vintage produced from Catalan white varieties in DO Montsant

wines.description: Entry-level DO Montsant white from Venus la Universal, blending organic Garnacha Blanca, Macabeo and Xarel-lo with fresh citrus and mineral character.

wines.name: Etim Negre

Rustic Catalan migas pair comfortably with this approachable cooperative red.

history.summary: The Cooperativa Falset Marca, founded in 1920, launched the Etim brand in the late 1990s to market their wines beyond bulk, with Etim Negre as the accessible entry point from the co-op's member-grower parcels in DO Montsant.

milestones.event: Cooperativa Falset Marca founded
Etim brand launched; Etim Negre first released

wines.description: Entry-level DO Montsant red from the Cooperativa Falset Marca's Etim range, offering ripe Garnacha and Cariñena fruit at an accessible price point.

wines.name: Etim Garnatxa Vermella
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in oak

The wine's soft profile makes a natural companion to regional charcuterie.
Hearty legume stew finds balance with the wine's ripe fruit and modest structure.

history.summary: Etim Garnatxa Vermella highlights the cooperative's focus on the Garnacha grape, the workhorse variety of the Falset and Montsant zone, vinified as a single varietal to showcase ripe fruit character.

milestones.event: First Etim Garnatxa Vermella single-varietal bottling released

wines.description: Single-varietal Garnacha from the Cooperativa Falset Marca's Etim range, made from member-grower parcels in DO Montsant showing warm, ripe red fruit.

wines.name: Etim Dolc Natural
wines.oak_regime: Aged in small oak casks

pairings.why: The wine's caramelised and custard notes match the classic Catalan creme brulee.
The wine's sweetness and oxidative depth make a natural match with pungent blue cheese.
Dark chocolate and almond resonance bridges the wine's dried-fruit sweetness.

history.summary: Etim Dolc Natural draws on the Catalan tradition of naturally sweet wines from sun-dried Garnacha, a style with roots in the region's historic Carthusian and monastic winemaking heritage.

milestones.event: Etim Dolc Natural first released by Cooperativa Falset Marca

wines.description: A traditional Catalan naturally sweet wine from the Cooperativa Falset Marca, produced from late-harvested Garnacha with dried-fruit richness and oxidative complexity.

wines.name: Galena

pairings.why: The wine's mineral and herb register complements slow-roasted lamb and Provencal herbs.

pairings.why: Wild game and aromatic herbs harmonise with the wine's iron and leather depth.
The cheese's crystalline salt amplifies the wine's dark fruit and spice.

history.summary: Clos Galena was established in the Poboleda area of DOQ Priorat, working with Garnacha, Cariñena and Syrah from llicorella slate terraces. The Galena bottling is the estate's premium expression, named after the galena mineral found in the region.

milestones.event: First Galena vintage produced

wines.description: Estate flagship of Clos Galena in Poboleda, DOQ Priorat, blending Garnacha, Cariñena and Syrah from llicorella slate with concentrated dark fruit and mineral depth.

wines.name: Tres Esteles

pairings.why: Rustic Catalan pork sausage and white beans pair naturally with this fresh, mineral Priorat.

pairings.dish: Tomato salad with olive oil and salt
pairings.why: Simple Catalan summer salad suits the wine's fresh red fruit and mineral freshness.
Classic tapas accompaniment matches the wine's garrigue and pepper notes.

history.summary: Tres Esteles, meaning Three Stars, is the accessible entry-level tier from Clos Galena, released to offer a more approachable expression of Poboleda Garnacha and Cariñena at a younger drinking window.

milestones.event: Tres Esteles first released as Clos Galena entry-level tier

wines.description: Entry-level DOQ Priorat from Clos Galena in Poboleda, offering fresh Garnacha and Cariñena red fruit with garrigue herbs and mineral freshness.

wines.name: Lo Givot

Game meat with savour and fat mirrors the wine's mineral and herb depth.
Smoky aged Basque sheep's cheese resonates with the wine's tobacco and slate character.

history.summary: Lo Givot is the principal wine of Celler del Pont, a small artisan producer based in the village of Porrera, the Cariñena heartland of DOQ Priorat. The cellar works exclusively with locally sourced old-vine parcels.

milestones.event: Lo Givot first vintage from Porrera parcels

wines.description: The flagship red of Celler del Pont in Porrera, DOQ Priorat, made from old Garnacha and Cariñena vines on llicorella with artisan winemaking.

wines.name: Tarot
wines.oak_regime: 6 months in used oak

Rustic Catalan stew suits the wine's approachable red-fruit character.
Classic Spanish bar snacks match the wine's garrigue and pepper notes.

history.summary: Tarot was introduced by Celler del Pont as an entry-level tier to complement Lo Givot, offering everyday drinking from Porrera's old-vine parcels.

milestones.event: Tarot first released as Celler del Pont entry-level wine

wines.description: Entry-level DOQ Priorat from Celler del Pont in Porrera, offering fresh Garnacha and Cariñena red fruit with mineral freshness.

wines.name: Les Pedrenyeres
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in used French oak foudre

pairings.why: Fatty, marbled Iberian pork fat and charred exterior complement the wine's iron and tannin.
Earthy truffle and savoury rice find resonance with the wine's mineral and herbal depth.

pairings.dish: Braised rabbit with olives and thyme
pairings.why: Catalan rabbit stew echoes the wine's herb and mineral savour.

history.summary: Les Pedrenyeres is the single-vineyard flagship of Orto Vins in El Lloar, named after the rocky, stony terrain of the plot. Joan Asens works with very old Cariñena vines on steep llicorella terraces.

milestones.event: First Les Pedrenyeres vintage from old Cariñena in El Lloar

wines.description: Single-vineyard Cariñena-led DOQ Priorat from Orto Vins in El Lloar, showing old-vine mineral depth and firm structure from steep llicorella terraces.

wines.name: Les Aubaguetes
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in used French oak

pairings.why: Classic Catalan rabbit echoes the wine's herb and mineral savour.
Smoky charred vegetables suit the wine's garrigue and mineral register.
The Catalan-Roussillon cinnamon-spiced meatballs pair with Garnacha's warm red fruit.

history.summary: Les Aubaguetes is Orto Vins's mid-range offering, drawing from a broader parcel selection in El Lloar on llicorella, offering more approachable drinking than the single-vineyard Les Pedrenyeres.

milestones.event: Les Aubaguetes first released as Orto Vins mid-range

wines.description: Mid-range DOQ Priorat from Orto Vins in El Lloar, blending Garnacha and Cariñena with garrigue herbs and mineral character from llicorella.

wines.name: Cecilio Negre
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in French and Spanish oak barrique

pairings.why: The wine's dark fruit and firm tannins cut through rich marbled beef.
Earthy mushroom and creamy filling contrast with the wine's mineral and herb notes.

pairings.dish: Pa amb tomaquet with aged cheese
pairings.why: Rustic Catalan bread and aged cheese echo the wine's earthy, mineral character.

history.summary: Cellers Cecilio is one of the older producer cellars in Gratallops, vinifying old Garnacha and Cariñena vines with traditional methods. Cecilio Negre is the principal red expression of the cellar.

milestones.event: Cecilio Negre first released under the current label

wines.description: The principal red of Cellers Cecilio in Gratallops, DOQ Priorat, made from old Garnacha and Cariñena on llicorella with characteristic dark fruit and mineral structure.

wines.name: Cecilio Blanc
wines.oak_regime: Aged in used French oak barrique

pairings.why: Delicate white fish pairs with the wine's saline mineral and citrus freshness.
Vinegar-pickled anchovies find a match in the wine's saline mineral and acidic freshness.
Tangy cheese and walnut echo the wine's almond and citrus notes.

history.summary: Cecilio Blanc was added as the white expression of Cellers Cecilio, utilising old Garnacha Blanca vines in Gratallops to explore the white wine potential of the DOQ Priorat appellation.

milestones.event: Cecilio Blanc first produced from old Garnacha Blanca in Gratallops

wines.description: Single-varietal Garnacha Blanca from Cellers Cecilio in Gratallops, DOQ Priorat, showing stone fruit, beeswax and saline mineral character.

wines.name: Mas d'en Compte
wines.oak_regime: 12 months in used French oak foudre

pairings.dish: Slow-cooked leg of lamb with garlic and rosemary
pairings.why: The wine's firm tannins and mineral core complement the lamb's fat and herb flavours.

pairings.dish: Wild mushroom and black truffle rice
pairings.why: Earthy truffle and mushroom rice echo the wine's mineral and tobacco depth.
Rich pork sausage pairs naturally with the wine's dark fruit and mineral edge.

history.summary: Mas d'en Compte is the flagship red of the Porrera-based producer of the same name, working the Porrera Vi de Vila zone within DOQ Priorat. Cariñena is the dominant variety in this historic Porrera terroir.

milestones.event: Mas d'en Compte first vintage produced

wines.description: Cariñena-led red from Mas d'en Compte in the Porrera Vi de Vila zone, DOQ Priorat, with characteristic iron-ore mineral depth and old-vine concentration.

wines.name: Mas d'en Compte Vi de Vila
wines.oak_regime: 8 months in used oak

pairings.dish: Carn d'olla (Catalan meat and vegetable stew)
pairings.why: Rustic Catalan slow-pot stew pairs comfortably with this village-level Priorat.
Simple charcoal lamb and the wine's red fruit and mineral freshness are natural companions.
Sheep's cheese with quince paste echo the wine's fruit and mineral notes.

history.summary: The Porrera Vi de Vila bottling from Mas d'en Compte represents the village-level classification within DOQ Priorat, offering an accessible expression of Porrera's Cariñena and Garnacha character.

milestones.event: Mas d'en Compte Vi de Vila first released at village-level classification

wines.description: Village-level DOQ Priorat from Mas d'en Compte in the Porrera Vi de Vila zone, showing fresh Cariñena and Garnacha character at accessible pricing.

wines.name: Mas d'en Compte Blanc
wines.oak_regime: Fermented in used oak barrique

pairings.dish: Bacalao al pil-pil (Basque salt cod in olive oil)
pairings.why: Rich cod emulsion and saline character mirror the wine's waxy texture and saline finish.
Shellfish with garlic emulsion pairs with the wine's saline mineral and almond notes.
Bitter white asparagus and the wine's almond and citrus notes are complementary.

history.summary: Mas d'en Compte Blanc was added to explore the white wine character of Porrera's Garnacha Blanca vines, contributing a white expression to a portfolio traditionally focused on red wines.

milestones.event: Mas d'en Compte Blanc first vintage from Porrera Garnacha Blanca

wines.description: Garnacha Blanca white from Mas d'en Compte in the Porrera Vi de Vila zone, showing waxy stone fruit and saline mineral character typical of Priorat whites.

wines.name: Les Eres Vinyes Velles
wines.oak_regime: 14 months in used French foudre

pairings.why: Bold tannins and minerality pair with the weight and marbling of a long-bone beef cut.
Crystalline salt in aged cheese and quince sweetness counterpoint the wine's iron and tannin.
Dense legume stew and pork fat soften the wine's angular structure.

history.summary: Les Eres is the primary single-vineyard expression of Celler Joan Simo in Porrera, drawing from Vinyes Velles (old vines) of Cariñena and Garnacha. Porrera is considered the heartland of old-vine Cariñena in DOQ Priorat.

milestones.event: Les Eres Vinyes Velles first vintage produced

wines.description: Single-vineyard old-vine Cariñena and Garnacha from Celler Joan Simo in Porrera, DOQ Priorat, showing angular iron-ore minerality and cellar-worthy structure.

wines.name: Les Eres Anfora
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in clay amphora

pairings.why: The wine's bright cherry and iron notes lift and complement duck fat and fruit sauce.

pairings.dish: Lamb chops with mint and garlic
pairings.why: Herb-seasoned grilled lamb harmonises with the wine's mineral and garrigue notes.
Simple Mediterranean roasted tomatoes echo the wine's freshness and mineral purity.

history.summary: Les Eres Anfora was introduced by Celler Joan Simo in 2014 as an experiment in clay-vessel winemaking, using the same old Cariñena vineyard as Les Eres Vinyes Velles but with no wood contact, aiming for maximum terroir transparency.

milestones.event: Les Eres Anfora first amphora-aged Cariñena released

wines.description: Amphora-aged single-varietal Cariñena from Celler Joan Simo in Porrera, DOQ Priorat, offering pure mineral expression without wood influence.

wines.name: Les Sorts Sycar
wines.oak_regime: 10 months in used French and American oak

pairings.why: Iberian pork and charcoal complement the wine's dark fruit and savoury mineral notes.
White bean and pork sausage stew pairs naturally with this earthy Montsant red.
Sweet-salty cheese board echoes the wine's fruit and mineral character.

history.summary: Les Sorts is the flagship range of Celler el Masroig, the El Masroig cooperative founded in 1917. Sycar is named for the Cariñena grape inverted, celebrating the cooperative's expertise with this native variety.

milestones.event: Celler el Masroig cooperative founded
Les Sorts Sycar first released as cooperative's Cariñena-focused premium tier

wines.description: Cariñena-dominant DO Montsant from the Celler el Masroig cooperative, showcasing the grape's mineral depth and dark-fruit character from Montsant slate.
